LABORATORY INVESTIGATION REPORT
LIR Number: LIR-2025-0023
Initiated By: David Kim, Senior Analyst
Date Initiated: 2025-05-02
Product: Lisinopril Tablets 10mg
Batch Number: 2025-LIS-047
Test: Assay (HPLC)
OOS Result: 93.8% (Specification: 90.0%–110.0% — RESULT IS WITHIN SPECIFICATION)
Reason for Investigation: Result below internal alert limit of 95.0%

--- PLANTED DEFICIENCIES ---
DEFICIENCY-1 [L5/oos_investigation_trigger_defined]: Trigger is internal alert limit, not specification — investigation standard unclear
DEFICIENCY-2 [L4/vague_language]: "acceptable" used without defining the specific threshold for retesting
DEFICIENCY-3 [L3/evidence_citation]: Phase I and Phase II conclusions not supported by specific data citations
DEFICIENCY-4 [L4/alcoa_original]: Mentions "corrected chromatogram" without documenting why original was corrected
DEFICIENCY-5 [L2/missing_element]: No statement on whether retained sample was tested
DEFICIENCY-6 [L2/missing_element]: Batch disposition recommendation missing from conclusion
--- END PLANTED DEFICIENCIES ---

1. PURPOSE

This Laboratory Investigation Report documents the investigation into an out-of-specification (OOS) / out-of-alert (OOA) result for the Assay determination of Lisinopril Tablets 10mg, Batch 2025-LIS-047. The result of 93.8% is within the specification range of 90.0%–110.0% but falls below the internal alert threshold of 95.0%, triggering this formal investigation.

2. ORIGINAL TEST RESULT

Test: Assay by HPLC (Method ATM-CHEM-0045)
Analyst: D. Kim
Date of Analysis: May 2, 2025
Original Result: 93.8%
Specification: 90.0%–110.0% (PASS)
Internal Alert Limit: 95.0% (ALERT TRIGGERED)
Instrument: HPLC-07

Note: A corrected chromatogram was used for the final calculation. The original chromatogram integration was adjusted.

3. PHASE I INVESTIGATION — LABORATORY INVESTIGATION

3.1 Objective
Determine whether the OOA result is due to an assignable laboratory error.

3.2 System Suitability Review
System suitability results were reviewed and found to be acceptable.

3.3 Analyst Interview
The analyst was interviewed and reported no unusual occurrences during the analysis.

3.4 Standard Preparation Review
Reference standard preparation was reviewed and appeared to be correct.

3.5 Sample Preparation Review
Sample preparation documentation was reviewed. No errors were identified.

3.6 Data Review
Raw data files were reviewed. The data appeared to be complete and acceptable.

3.7 Phase I Conclusion
No assignable laboratory error was identified. The OOA result appears to be genuine. Investigation proceeds to Phase II.

4. PHASE II INVESTIGATION — MANUFACTURING INVESTIGATION

4.1 Batch Record Review
The batch manufacturing record for Batch 2025-LIS-047 was reviewed. Process parameters appeared to be within acceptable ranges.

4.2 In-Process Testing Review
In-process assay results were reviewed. Results showed acceptable variability.

4.3 Raw Material Review
Certificates of Analysis for API and excipients were reviewed. All incoming material testing results were acceptable.

4.4 Equipment Review
Manufacturing equipment used for Batch 2025-LIS-047 was reviewed. Calibration and qualification status appeared to be current.

4.5 Environmental Conditions Review
Environmental monitoring data for the manufacturing area was reviewed. No excursions were noted.

4.6 Phase II Conclusion
No manufacturing-related assignable cause was identified for the OOA result. The result of 93.8% is genuine and within specification.

5. RETEST RESULTS

Per SOP-QC-015, a retest was conducted by a second analyst:
Retest Result 1 (Analyst: R. Patel): 94.2%
Retest Result 2 (Analyst: R. Patel): 94.6%
Average of all results: 94.2%

All results are within specification (90.0%–110.0%). Average result remains below the 95.0% alert limit.

6. CONCLUSION

The OOA result of 93.8% for Assay of Lisinopril Tablets 10mg, Batch 2025-LIS-047 is confirmed. No assignable laboratory or manufacturing cause was identified. All results are within the specification range. The batch meets established release specifications.

7. REGULATORY CITATIONS

21 CFR 211.192 — Production record review and investigation
21 CFR 211.165 — Testing and release for distribution
FDA Guidance: Investigating Out-of-Specification (OOS) Test Results for Pharmaceutical Production (2006)

8. APPROVALS

Analyst: David Kim _________________ Date: May 5, 2025
QC Supervisor: _________________ Date: _________
QA Review: _________________ Date: _________
